_NOTE: DEMO/SAMPLE DATA — not a live scan. This sandbox has no Docker or network access, so this file mimics Trivy's real JSON schema with illustrative CVE-style IDs (prefixed DEMO-) to test compliance_mapper.py end-to-end. When you run the actual GitHub Actions workflow, Trivy will overwrite this with real scan results from real CVEs.

Vulnerabilities.Title: openssl: TLS handshake remote code execution
Vulnerabilities.Description: A flaw in TLS certificate parsing allows remote code execution via crafted handshake messages.

Vulnerabilities.Title: glibc: heap buffer overflow in dynamic loader
Vulnerabilities.Description: Buffer overflow in the dynamic loader allows privilege escalation to root.

Vulnerabilities.Title: zlib: heap-based buffer overflow in inflate()
Vulnerabilities.Description: Crafted compressed input can trigger a buffer overflow during decompression.

Vulnerabilities.Title: curl: TLS session reuse authentication bypass
Vulnerabilities.Description: Improper session cache validation can allow auth bypass under specific reuse conditions.

Vulnerabilities.Title: ncurses: out-of-bounds read in terminal capability parser
Vulnerabilities.Description: Malformed terminfo entries can cause an out-of-bounds read.

Vulnerabilities.Title: tar: denial of service via crafted archive
Vulnerabilities.Description: A crafted archive can cause excessive resource consumption during extraction.

Vulnerabilities.Title: gzip: minor information disclosure via error messages
Vulnerabilities.Description: Verbose error output may leak partial file path information.

Vulnerabilities.Title: Flask: improper session cookie validation
Vulnerabilities.Description: Session cookies are not properly validated, allowing forged authentication tokens.

Vulnerabilities.Title: Werkzeug: debugger denial of service
Vulnerabilities.Description: The interactive debugger can be triggered remotely under certain misconfigurations.
